frontend-dev.description: React/TypeScript developer specializing in UI/UX implementation with MUI

backend-dev.description: Node.js/Express developer specializing in API design and image processing

image-processing.description: Computer vision and image processing expert for filet crochet pattern generation

ui-ux.description: UI/UX designer for MUI-based design system

setup.description: Run full project setup (React + Node + Docker + Terraform)
setup.prompt: Execute the project setup sequence: 1) Run pnpm-workspace skill, 2) Run react-setup skill, 3) Run node-setup skill, 4) Run docker-setup skill, 5) Run terraform-setup skill. Report progress after each step.

test.description: Run all tests and verify 80% coverage
test.prompt: Run all tests: pnpm test:web, pnpm test:api. Verify coverage is above 80%. If not, report which files need more tests.

coverage.prompt: Generate coverage reports for both frontend and backend. Display the coverage percentages and identify any files below 80% threshold.

backlog.prompt: Read and display backlog.md. If there are tasks in progress, report current status. If completing a task, verify it has tests and coverage before marking as completed.

commit.description: Generate conventional commit message and commit changes
commit.prompt: Use the commit skill. 1) Run `git status` and `git diff --cached` to see staged changes. 2) Determine the correct type (feat/fix/docs/style/refactor/test/chore/ci/infra) and scope (web/api/shared-types/image-processing/ui-components/devops/infra). 3) Generate a conventional commit message in format: `type(scope): subject`. 4) Show the proposed message to the user and ask for confirmation. 5) If confirmed, run `git commit -m "<message>"`. If there are multiple logical changes, suggest splitting into multiple commits or using `git commit` with a body.

branch.description: Create a new feature branch from main
branch.prompt: Use the branch skill. 1) Run `git status` to check current branch. 2) If on main, run `git pull origin main` to get latest changes. 3) Determine the branch name based on the task type (feat/fix/docs/refactor/test/chore) and a brief description. 4) Use the pattern: `type/description`. 5) Show the proposed branch name to the user and ask for confirmation. 6) If confirmed, run `git checkout -b <branch-name>`. If not on main, warn the user and ask if they want to switch to main first.

pr.description: Create a pull request from current branch to main
pr.prompt: Use the pr skill. 1) Run `git status` to check current branch. 2) If on main, warn the user and exit. 3) Check if there are uncommitted changes. If so, prompt to commit first. 4) Run `git push origin <branch-name>`. 5) Generate a PR title using conventional commit format: `type(scope): description`. 6) Generate a PR description using the template in .opencode/skills/pr/SKILL.md. 7) Show the proposed PR title and description to the user. 8) Ask for confirmation. 9) If confirmed, create the PR using `gh pr create --title "<title>" --body "<body>" --base main`. 10) Show the PR URL.
